figures.caption: Table 1. Results on Llama-2-7B-Chat after sequential adaptation (Medical → Legal → Code). Safety Score is the composite of HarmBench, TruthfulQA, and BBQ (Eq. 4.1). All values: mean ± std over 5 seeds. Best in bold, second underlined.

Figure 1. SafeAnchor pipeline. SSI identifies safety-critical LoRA directions via Fisher eigendecomposition. OSCA projects domain gradients orthogonally during training. CSM triggers corrective replay if safety degrades. The subspace is incrementally updated after each domain.

Table 5. Safety score after each adaptation on Llama-2-7B-Chat, extended to T=5. SafeAnchor’s slope holds at approximately 2 pts/step; Standard LoRA decelerates only because the refusal rate approaches a floor.
Table 3. Ablation study on Llama-2-7B-Chat showing the contribution of each SafeAnchor component. Mean ± std over 5 seeds.
Figure 2. Safety score trajectory across sequential domain adaptations on Llama-2-7BChat. SafeAnchor prevents the compounding erosion exhibited by all baselines.
Table 11. Complete hyperparameter specification for SafeAnchor. All main-text numbers use these defaults unless explicitly varied.

Table 12. Per-phase wall-clock on Llama-2-7B-Chat. "Share of pipeline" is each phase’s time divided by the full SafeAnchor pipeline time (7h 47min); percentages sum to 100% (rounding). In addition to the per-domain components shown, one pipeline run incurs ∼17 min of fixed overhead (initial s0 evaluation, final full-eval suite, and I/O). The OSCA per-step overhead (+17.8%) is measured against unconstrained-LoRA training-step time.
